nodes.title: Louis IX: King,Crusader,Saint
nodes.description: Topics examining themes in Medieval European history. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: Twelve hours of History; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Twelve hours of History; minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: History of China and Japan
nodes.description: An introductory survey of the history of Chinese and Japanese civilizations from their Neolithic origins until the twentieth century.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: History of Poland
nodes.description: History of the Polish people and Polish state from the tenth century to the present. Strong emphasis on the twentieth century. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Nazism and Fascism
nodes.description: Topics examining themes in Holocaust Studies and Modern European history. Representative topics: The Holocaust & Memory; Auschwitz; The Holocaust in Poland. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Credit not awarded for both HST 4790 and HST 5700. Prerequisites: Twelve hours of History; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Modern Mexico
nodes.description: Topics examining Mexican history. Representative topics: Modern Mexico. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Modern Latin American History
nodes.description: Comparative survey concentrating on Latin America from the independence movements to the present with emphasis on cultural, political, and economic development and US intervention.

nodes.title: Nazism and Fascism
nodes.description: Exploration of topics in European history. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Credit not awarded for both HST 5700 and HST 4690 to HST 4790. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student. 12 hours History; Graduate History students only; Juniors and Seniors register for HST 4790A

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Required of all candidates for the M.A. who are writing a thesis. Normally arranged for two semesters at three hours each.

nodes.title: Medieval Mystics & Heretics
nodes.description: Exploration of the explosion of new religious ideas that characterized the period from 1100 to 1500 and the Church's response to these challenges. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: US History to 1865
nodes.description: Survey of American history from the pre-Revolutionary period through the Civil War era. Credit not awarded for both HST 1160 and HST 1610.

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Roman History/Civilization II
nodes.description: Topics examining Ancient history. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours in History or Classics.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.  Students who have taken CLAS 1320, CLAS 2320, HST 1705, HST 2705 in fall 2025 are welcome to register. Please email the instructor if you have questions about registration.

nodes.title: US Civil War - Global Context
nodes.description: Topics examining the diplomatic history of the United States and/or its colonial antecedents, usually with an emphasis on law. Representative topics: Treaties and International Law; US Civil War in Global Context. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: Three hours of History. Pace students by permission and override

nodes.title: Canadian American Relations
nodes.description: Topics examining the history of Canada. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: History Methods
nodes.description: Investigation of the theory and practice of history through critique of historians' methods, analysis of primary sources, and development of the research and writing skills necessary for constructing historical arguments. Prerequisites: History major; three hours in History; Sophomore standing recommended.
nodes.prereq_expression: {"type": "AND", "operands": [{"type": "course", "code": "History major"}, {"type": "OR", "operands": [{"type": "course", "code": "three hours in History"}, {"type": "course", "code": "three hours History"}]}]}
nodes.other_requirements: Sophomore standing recommended. History majors only; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students by permission and override. The class is for History majors, but History minors and Secondary Education Social Studies majors are welcome to request overrides if there are seats available in the week before classes start and/or during the add/drop period.

nodes.title: Exploring Abenaki History
nodes.description: Exploration of topics in the history of the Americas. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Credit not awarded for both HST 5500 and HST 4470 to HST 4690. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student. 12 hours History; Graduate History students only; Juniors and Seniors register for HST 4500A; Co-located with HST 4500A

nodes.title: Graduate Historiography
nodes.description: Historical methods, philosophy of history, and the history of history writing. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student. History Graduate Students Only

nodes.title: Modern Germany
nodes.description: Political, cultural, and social history of Germany from unification in 1871 through the Wilhemine empire, Weimar Republic, Nazi era, and postwar period. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: The Holocaust
nodes.description: Study of the background, events, and aftermath of the Holocaust in Nazi Germany and Europe under German control. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Nonhuman Histories
nodes.description: Topics examining themes in Global history. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Twelve hours of History; minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: Interracial Activism in the US
nodes.description: Topics examining the experiences of Black people in the United States and/or its colonial antecedents. Representative topics: African-American History to 1865; African-American History since 1865. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Global History to 1500
nodes.description: The development and cross-fertilization of civilizations in Eurasia, Africa, and the Americas from about 3500 BCE to AD 1500.

nodes.title: 20th C Euro History in Film
nodes.description: Topics examining the history of Europe. Representative topic: Twentieth-century European History in Film.  May repeat be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.

nodes.title: World War II
nodes.description: Causes, conduct, and consequences of global war from 1931 to 1945, including social, economic, political, and diplomatic as well as military aspects. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: US Women's History
nodes.description: Topics examining the experiences of women in the United States and/or its colonial antecedents. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History or Gender, Sexuality, and Women's Studies minor.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Directed individual study of areas not appropriately covered by existing courses. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Graduate History Students only

nodes.title: Ottoman Empire in Comparative
nodes.description: Exploration of topics in global history. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Credit not awarded for both HST 5300 and HST 4210 to HST 4447. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student. 12 hours History; Graduate History students only; Juniors and Seniors register for Hst 4300 A; Co-located with Hst 4300 A; Total combined enrollment is 20

nodes.title: North American Indian History
nodes.description: Surveys Native North American history across regions of the continent that became Canada and the United States from pre- contact to the present, with emphasis on Indian-European interaction.

nodes.title: Africa Before Colonialism
nodes.description: Topics examining the history of Africa. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Modern Japan
nodes.description: Transition from tradition to modernity in Japan from the Meiji Restoration, 1868 to the present. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Roman History/Civilization I
nodes.description: Topics examining Ancient history. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.

nodes.title: Comparative Slavery
nodes.description: History of slavery from a comparative perspective, including Classical Antiquity, Islam and the Middle East, Africa, Latin America, and the Southern United States. Credit not awarded for both HST 4210 and HST 5300. Prerequisites: Twelve hours of History; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: TA:20th Century Europe in Film
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: LASP: Visualizing India
nodes.description: Intensive course in a broad disciplinary area (humanities, social sciences, arts, or natural sciences). Part of an integrated first-year experience in which students take 2-4 classes exploring aesthetic, humanistic, social, linguistic, environmental, or scientific issues. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Co-requisite: Enrollment in the appropriate Liberal Arts Scholars Program.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisite: Enrollment in the appropriate Liberal Arts Scholars Program. LASP Humanities Scholars only; Instructor permission only

nodes.title: Love and Death in Europe
nodes.description: Topics examining European history. Representative topics: Capetian France; World War I in Europe; Twentieth-century Europe. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Global Witchcraft
nodes.description: Topics examining historical themes and questions on a global scale. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Ottoman Empire in Comparative
nodes.description: Topics examining the history of the Middle East. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: Twelve hours of History; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing. PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: US History since 1865
nodes.description: Survey of US history from the Civil War era.

nodes.title: Drugs in the Americas
nodes.description: Examination of the history of drugs and drug trafficking in Latin America from the colonial era to the present. Above all, this is a course about the impact of drugs and drug trafficking on people: thus, this course is also about violence, racism, inequality, poverty, migration, discrimination, criminalization, incarceration, and human rights. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. History graduate students only

nodes.title: History of Magic
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: The West in East Asia
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Early Europe
nodes.description: Survey of European history, 500-1648.

nodes.title: History of Modern Middle East
nodes.description: Offers an historical understanding of social and political change in the Middle East during the nineteenth and twentieth centuries. Prerequisite: Three hours of History.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE Students by permission and override

nodes.title: FYS: History of Assassinations
nodes.description: Intensive first-year seminar focused on specific themes and/or disciplinary perspectives. Emphasis on developing critical reading and writing skills, substantive revision, information literacy, and analytical thinking. First-year seminars are frequently organized to meet one of the disciplinary Catamount Core requirements. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.

nodes.title: Exploring Abenaki History
nodes.description: Topics examining historical themes and questions in the history of the Americas, with a particular emphasis on places beyond the United States. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: Twelve hours of History; minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Instructor permission required
